name: memory-graph
description: Obsidian-style force-directed knowledge graph over memories + agents + skills + tools, with agent to skill/tool cross-links. Portable renderer + wired adapter; ships its own scoped styles.
notes: Frontend-only slice — NO new table, NO new Convex function. The wired adapter (hooks/use-graph-data) reads existing queries memory.listMemories + agentDefs.list + agentDefs.listSkillsRegistry + agentDefs.listToolRegistry and persists via memory.addMemory; it depends on the `memory` slice's backend. The pure <MemoryGraph> renderer is Convex-free (feed it any GraphData), manages its own force sim + pan/zoom/drag imperatively, and injects its own scoped <style>, so it drops into any project. Consumer wiring = import { MemoryGraphPanel } and add a nav entry. DEVIATION(rr): this slice ships its own CSS as a scoped <style> string instead of reusing globals.css classes — deliberate, because the graph is ~350 lines of bespoke canvas styling and self-containment is the point of a portable slice. Node types: core, cluster, memory, agent, skill, tool.
